name: TAO 4h State Change Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: directional improvement appears after a pullback, fast structure is reclaimed, that reclaim holds on the following bar, and expansion resumes in the underlying uptrend
thesis: Cycle 72 explicitly calls for state-change momentum after regime improvement and post-break hold. TAO 4h is the natural home lane because it is the only allowed lane currently flagged TREND_UP. The edge is not first-break chasing. It is buying the moment when a pullback hands control back to the trend and the reclaim actually holds for one more bar.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h reclaim-hold sequences remain too sparse or repeatedly fail before modest expansion, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A trend-up asset pulls back, then improves directionally by reclaiming fast structure and proving that reclaim with one more hold bar before the next leg expands.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a live trend-up context, pullback recoveries that reclaim and then hold fast structure often represent the point where weak sellers are exhausted and continuation resumes. The hold requirement filters some one-bar bounce noise without turning the idea into a ceremonial sparse chain.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it has the strongest trend-up contamination in the basket and enough amplitude for reclaim-hold continuation to matter.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: pullback depth tolerance and stop sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Late entries after too much recovery can become local exhaustion buys inside a maturing leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the concept trades but the runner underperforms, move more size to the first partial before changing the reclaim grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of pullback recoveries, many first-target wins, and a smaller number of strong runners when the trend leg truly resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density with positive post-cost first-target behavior and at least one cross-lane keeps the same reclaim-hold structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated hold confirmations that fail before first scale-out.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because the hold is one-bar confirmation only

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit live trend-up lane for reclaim-hold continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reclaim-hold expansions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h entries prove too sparse or turn into late exhaustion buys.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with several continuation bursts and a few larger runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim-hold grammar survives on a weaker, structurally different lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h preserves enough reclaim-hold events to show portability.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h is too weak or too trend-down contaminated for this continuation idea.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count and smaller continuation legs than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether pure chop suppresses the same continuation grammar.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints a tradable version of the reclaim-hold idea.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept depends on TAO's trend context.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse but orderly continuation attempts with smaller carry.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.12 * ATR_14 and close remains >= EMA_20
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.12 * ATR_14 and close remains <= EMA_20
